Code 1: "Reddened area on sacrum, non-blanchable"
Code 4: "Multiple reddened areas: sacrum, both heels, left troch, right troch"

---
M1324 - STAGE OF MOST PROBLEMATIC PRESSURE ULCER (STAGEABLE)

"Stage of Most Problematic Unhealed Pressure Ulcer/Injury that is Stageable"

EXCLUDES: Unstageables (non-removable dressing, slough/eschar, deep tissue injury)

CODES:
1 = Stage 1 (most problematic is Stage 1)
2 = Stage 2 (most problematic is Stage 2)
3 = Stage 3 (most problematic is Stage 3)
4 = Stage 4 (most problematic is Stage 4)
NA = No pressure ulcers OR only unstageables

DECISION LOGIC:
1. Has any stageable pressure ulcers (Stage 1-4)? NO → Code NA
2. YES: Which is most problematic?
   - Consider: Severity, size, location, healing progress
   - Generally: Stage 4 > Stage 3 > Stage 2 > Stage 1
   - Code the stage of the WORST one

PATTERNS:
Has Stage 2 and Stage 3 → Code 3 (Stage 3 is worse)
Has only Stage 1 → Code 1
Has only unstageables, no stageable ulcers → Code NA
No pressure ulcers at all → Code NA

---
M1330 - STASIS ULCER PRESENCE

"Does this patient have a Stasis Ulcer?"

STASIS ULCER = Venous insufficiency ulcer (lower leg wound due to venous disease)

CODES:
0 = No → Skip to M1340 (Surgical Wound)
1 = Yes, BOTH observable and unobservable
2 = Yes, observable ONLY
3 = Yes, unobservable ONLY (non-removable dressing) → Skip to M1340

DEFINITIONS:
Observable = Can see/assess wound
Unobservable = Covered by non-removable dressing, cannot see

DECISION:
- No stasis ulcers → 0
- Has stasis ulcer(s), all can be seen → 2
- Has stasis ulcer(s), all covered → 3
- Has some visible, some covered → 1

PATTERNS:
Code 0: "No venous ulcers", "No stasis ulcers"
Code 1: "Left leg medial ulcer visible, right leg lateral ulcer under compression wrap"
Code 2: "Medial ankle ulcer, visible and assessed"
Code 3: "Stasis ulcer present but under non-removable Unna boot"

---
M1332 - NUMBER OF OBSERVABLE STASIS ULCERS

Only complete if M1330 = 1 or 2 (has observable stasis ulcers)

CODES:
1 = One
2 = Two
3 = Three
4 = Four or more

Count only OBSERVABLE stasis ulcers (can see/assess)

PATTERNS:
"One medial leg ulcer" → Code 1
"Bilateral leg ulcers, both visible" → Code 2
"Multiple venous ulcers on left leg (5 total)" → Code 4

---
M1334 - STATUS OF MOST PROBLEMATIC STASIS ULCER (OBSERVABLE)

Only complete if M1330 = 1 or 2 (has observable stasis ulcers)

CODES:
1 = Fully granulating (entire wound bed is healthy red granulation tissue)
2 = Early/partial granulation (some granulation, not complete coverage)
3 = Not healing (no granulation, slough, poor appearance)

HEALING ASSESSMENT:
- Granulation tissue = healthy pink/red, bumpy/grainy appearance
- Fully granulating = ready for epithelialization
- Early/partial = healing in progress
- Not healing = stalled, deteriorating, or infected appearance

DECISION:
Select status of MOST PROBLEMATIC observable stasis ulcer
If multiple: choose worst one

PATTERNS:
Code 1: "Wound bed 100% red granulation tissue"
Code 2: "Wound bed 50% granulation, 50% slough"
Code 3: "Wound bed covered with yellow slough, no granulation"

---
M1340 - SURGICAL WOUND PRESENCE

"Does this patient have a Surgical Wound?"

SURGICAL WOUND = Post-operative incision/wound

CODES:
0 = No → Skip to N0415 (next section)
1 = Yes, at least one observable surgical wound → Continue to M1342
2 = Yes, but not observable due to non-removable dressing → Skip to N0415

DEFINITION:
Surgical wound from recent surgery requiring monitoring/treatment

OBSERVABLE = Can see/assess wound

DECISION:
- No surgical wounds → 0
- Has surgical wound(s), can see at least one → 1
- Has surgical wound(s), all covered by non-removable dressing → 2

PATTERNS:
Code 0: "No recent surgery", "No surgical wounds"
Code 1: "Post-op hip incision, healing well"
Code 2: "Abdominal incision under surgical dressing, cannot remove per MD orders"

---
M1342 - STATUS OF MOST PROBLEMATIC SURGICAL WOUND (OBSERVABLE)

Only complete if M1340 = 1 (has observable surgical wound)

CODES:
0 = Newly epithelialized (surface covered with new skin, closed)
1 = Fully granulating (open but entire wound bed is healthy granulation)
2 = Early/partial granulation (open, some granulation but not complete)
3 = Not healing (open, no granulation, deteriorating, infected)

HEALING STAGES:
- Newly epithelialized = Closed, pink new skin covering
- Fully granulating = Open, ready to close, healthy red tissue throughout
- Early/partial granulation = Open, healing in progress, mixed appearance
- Not healing = Open, poor/no healing, concerning appearance

DECISION:
Select status of MOST PROBLEMATIC observable surgical wound
If multiple: choose worst one

PATTERNS:
Code 0: "Incision well-approximated, closed, no drainage"
Code 1: "Open surgical wound, 100% granulation tissue"
Code 2: "Dehisced incision, 40% granulation, some slough"
Code 3: "Wound dehiscence with purulent drainage, no granulation"

---
WOUND INTEGRATION RULES

SKIP LOGIC:
- M1306=0 → Skip M1311, go to M1322
- M1330=0 → Skip M1332/M1334, go to M1340
- M1330=3 (unobservable only) → Skip M1332/M1334, go to M1340
- M1340=0 or 2 → Skip M1342, end section

WOUND TYPE DISTINCTIONS:

PRESSURE ULCER:
- Due to pressure/shear over bony prominence
- Staged 1-4 or unstageable
- Common locations: Sacrum, coccyx, heels, trochanters, ischium

STASIS ULCER:
- Due to venous insufficiency
- Lower leg (typically medial ankle/gaiter area)
- Associated with edema, hemosiderin staining, lipodermatosclerosis
- NOT staged (use healing status instead)

SURGICAL WOUND:
- Post-operative incision or wound
- May be closed (healing by primary intention) or open (secondary/tertiary)
- NOT staged (use healing status instead)

SAME WOUND, DIFFERENT TYPES:
A wound can only be ONE type:
- Pressure ulcer on surgical site → Code as pressure ulcer (use staging)
- Surgical incision that dehisced → Still surgical wound (use healing status)
- Venous ulcer over bony prominence → Determine primary etiology

---
HEALING STATUS DEFINITIONS

FOR STASIS AND SURGICAL WOUNDS:

NEWLY EPITHELIALIZED (Code 0):
- Only for surgical wounds (M1342)
- Wound closed with new epithelial tissue
- Pink/red new skin covering surface
- No longer open

FULLY GRANULATING (Code 1):
- Wound bed 100% covered with granulation tissue
- Healthy pink/red bumpy appearance
- Ready for epithelialization/closure
- Open wound

EARLY/PARTIAL GRANULATION (Code 2):
- Granulation tissue present but incomplete
- Mixed wound bed: granulation + slough/eschar/other
- Healing in progress
- Open wound

NOT HEALING (Code 3):
- Minimal or no granulation tissue
- Slough, eschar, necrotic tissue present
- Signs of infection or deterioration
- Stalled or worsening
- Open wound

---
OBSERVABLE vs UNOBSERVABLE

OBSERVABLE:
- Wound can be seen and assessed
- Dressing can be removed for assessment
- Can evaluate wound bed, edges, size

UNOBSERVABLE:
- Covered by non-removable dressing/device
- Cannot be removed per physician orders
- Cannot assess wound characteristics
- Examples: Wound VAC, surgical dressing with strict orders, Unna boot

CODING IMPACT:
Unobservable wounds:
- Count in presence questions (M1306, M1330, M1340)
- Do NOT count in number or status questions (requires observation)
- Specifically coded as "unobservable only" options

---
COMMON SCENARIOS

SCENARIO 1: Multiple pressure ulcers
- Stage 2 sacral ulcer
- Stage 3 right heel ulcer
- Stage 1 left heel area
Coding: M1306=1, M1311 A1=1 B1=1 others=0, M1322=1, M1324=3

SCENARIO 2: Stasis ulcers
- Left medial ankle venous ulcer (observable, early granulation)
- Right leg under Unna boot (not observable)
Coding: M1330=1, M1332=1, M1334=2

SCENARIO 3: Post-surgical
- Abdominal incision, well-healed, closed
Coding: M1340=1, M1342=0

SCENARIO 4: Complex wounds
- Unstageable coccyx (covered with eschar)
- Stage 2 sacral ulcer
- Surgical hip incision (fully granulating, open)
Coding: M1306=1, M1311 A1=1 E1=1 others=0, M1324=2, M1340=1, M1342=1

SCENARIO 5: No wounds
- Intact skin, no ulcers or surgical wounds
Coding: M1306=0, M1322=0, M1324=NA, M1330=0, M1340=0

---
COMMON CODING ERRORS

M1306/M1311:
❌ Counting Stage 1 in M1306 → Stage 1 excluded, code in M1322
❌ Counting healed ulcers → Only unhealed/open wounds
❌ Counting same ulcer in multiple stages → One category per ulcer
❌ Not entering 0 for stages not present → Must enter 0

M1324:
❌ Coding unstageable ulcer → Only stageable (1-4)
❌ Not selecting worst/most problematic → Code highest stage if unclear
❌ Leaving blank when have Stage 1 only → Should code 1

M1330-M1334:
❌ Confusing arterial with venous ulcers → Stasis = venous only
❌ Staging stasis ulcers → Use healing status, not staging
❌ Not distinguishing observable → Affects number and status codes

M1340-M1342:
❌ Coding remote surgical history → Recent surgery requiring care
❌ Using staging for surgical wounds → Use healing status
❌ Coding "0" for open wounds → 0 is closed (epithelialized), open = 1/2/3

---
QUICK REFERENCE

PRESSURE ULCER STAGING:
Stage 1: Intact, non-blanchable red
Stage 2: Partial thickness, shallow
Stage 3: Full thickness, fat visible
Stage 4: Full thickness, bone/tendon/muscle
Unstageable: Cannot determine depth

WOUND HEALING STATUS:
0: Newly epithelialized (closed)
1: Fully granulating (100% granulation)
2: Early/partial granulation (mixed)
3: Not healing (poor/no granulation)

SKIP LOGIC:
M1306=0 → M1322
M1330=0 or 3 → M1340
M1340=0 or 2 → End section

---
END GUIDANCE
llm_prompt_template:
You are an Expert OASIS-E1 Clinician specializing in wound assessment. Code these items: {field_codes}
Return JSON array only.

---
CLINICAL SUMMARY: {clinician_summary}

PATIENT CONTEXT:
{patient_data}

ICD-10 CODES:
{icd10_data}

---
INFERENCE RULES

PERMITTED (medium/low confidence):
- Pressure ulcer presence from "Stage 2/3/4 ulcer", "Unstageable wound", "Pressure injury"
- Location from "Sacral", "Coccyx", "Heel", "Trochanter"
- Staging from wound descriptions: "Partial thickness" → Stage 2, "Bone visible" → Stage 4
- Stasis ulcer from "Venous ulcer", "Lower leg ulcer", "Medial ankle ulcer"
- Surgical wound from "Post-op incision", "Surgical site", "Recent surgery"
- Healing status from "Granulating", "Closed", "Slough present", "Not healing"
- Observable status from dressing descriptions

PROHIBITED:
- Staging without clear wound depth description
- Assuming healed ulcers are current/unhealed
- Confusing wound types (pressure vs stasis vs surgical)
- Counting wounds without documented evidence
- Guessing healing status without wound bed description
- Coding remote surgical history as current wound

STAGING INFERENCE:
"Shallow open ulcer over sacrum" → Stage 2
"Deep wound, fat visible, no bone" → Stage 3
"Wound with exposed bone" → Stage 4
"Wound covered with black eschar" → Unstageable-slough/eschar
"Red intact area over heel, non-blanchable" → Stage 1

HEALING INFERENCE:
"Incision well-approximated, closed" → Code 0 (epithelialized)
"Wound bed 100% beefy red granulation" → Code 1 (fully granulating)
"Mixed wound bed, some granulation some slough" → Code 2 (early/partial)
"Yellow slough covering wound bed" → Code 3 (not healing)

---
GUIDANCE: {oasis_guidance}

---
CODING PROTOCOL

M1306 PRESSURE ULCER PRESENCE (Stage 2+):
Any Stage 2, 3, 4, or Unstageable pressure ulcer?
- YES → Code 1, continue to M1311
- NO → Code 0, skip to M1322

M1311 NUMBER AT EACH STAGE (if M1306=1):
Count and enter number (0-99) for each:
A1: Stage 2 (partial thickness, shallow)
B1: Stage 3 (full thickness, fat visible, no bone/tendon/muscle)
C1: Stage 4 (full thickness, bone/tendon/muscle exposed)
D1: Unstageable - non-removable dressing
E1: Unstageable - slough/eschar covering
F1: Unstageable - deep tissue injury (purple/maroon)
Enter 0 for stages not present

M1322 STAGE 1 COUNT:
Count Stage 1 pressure injuries (intact, non-blanchable red):
0=Zero, 1=One, 2=Two, 3=Three, 4=Four or more

M1324 MOST PROBLEMATIC STAGEABLE:
If has any Stage 1-4 ulcers (not unstageable):
Code stage of worst: 1=Stage 1, 2=Stage 2, 3=Stage 3, 4=Stage 4
If only unstageables or no ulcers: Code NA

M1330 STASIS ULCER PRESENCE:
Venous insufficiency ulcers?
0=No → Skip to M1340
1=Yes, both observable and unobservable
2=Yes, observable only → Continue to M1332/M1334
3=Yes, unobservable only → Skip to M1340

M1332 NUMBER OBSERVABLE STASIS (if M1330=1 or 2):
Count observable only: 1=One, 2=Two, 3=Three, 4=Four or more

M1334 STATUS MOST PROBLEMATIC STASIS (if M1330=1 or 2):
Worst observable stasis ulcer status:
1=Fully granulating, 2=Early/partial granulation, 3=Not healing

M1340 SURGICAL WOUND PRESENCE:
Post-operative wounds?
0=No → End section
1=Yes, at least one observable → Continue to M1342
2=Yes, unobservable only → End section

M1342 STATUS MOST PROBLEMATIC SURGICAL (if M1340=1):
Worst observable surgical wound status:
0=Newly epithelialized (closed)
1=Fully granulating (100% granulation)
2=Early/partial granulation
3=Not healing

VALIDATION:
- Skip logic followed correctly
- Numbers entered for all M1311 stages (enter 0 if none)
- Same wound not counted in multiple categories
- Wound types distinguished (pressure vs stasis vs surgical)
- Observable vs unobservable correctly identified
- Healing status matches wound description

---
OUTPUT FORMAT

[
  {
    "item_code": "M1306|M1311A1|M1311B1|M1311C1|M1311D1|M1311E1|M1311F1|M1322|M1324|M1330|M1332|M1334|M1340|M1342",
    "value": "M1306/M1330/M1340: 0-3 | M1311: number 0-99 | M1322: 0-4 | M1324: 1-4 or NA | M1332: 1-4 | M1334/M1342: 0-3",
    "rationale": "Quote: '...' [Wound description, location, stage/status evidence. Count if multiple. State if inferred from wound description.]",
    "confidence": "high|medium|low"
  }
]

SPECIAL NOTES:
- M1306=0 → Skip M1311, go to M1322
- M1311: Enter 0 for all stages not present (do not leave blank)
- M1324: NA if no stageable ulcers (only unstageables or none)
- M1330=0 or 3 → Skip M1332/M1334
- M1340=0 or 2 → Skip M1342
- Pressure ulcers: Use staging (1-4 or unstageable)
- Stasis/surgical wounds: Use healing status (0-3), NOT staging
- Same wound cannot be both pressure and stasis - determine primary etiology
- Observable = can see/assess; Unobservable = covered by non-removable dressing

---
VALIDATION CHECKLIST:
✓ Valid JSON, no markdown
✓ {field_count} objects
✓ M1306: 0 or 1
✓ M1311: All six stages (A1-F1) have numbers (0 if none)
✓ M1311: Numbers match wound descriptions
✓ M1322: 0-4 based on Stage 1 count
✓ M1324: 1-4 (stageable only) or NA
✓ Skip logic: M1306=0 → M1311 skipped
✓ Skip logic: M1330=0 or 3 → M1332/M1334 skipped
✓ Skip logic: M1340=0 or 2 → M1342 skipped
✓ Wound types distinguished (pressure vs stasis vs surgical)
✓ Staging only for pressure ulcers
✓ Healing status for stasis and surgical wounds
✓ Observable vs unobservable correctly coded
✓ Most problematic = worst wound selected
✓ Evidence supports all wound counts and stages

Return JSON only.

rag_query: medication, caregiver, anxiety, swallow, surgical, N0415F, prefilled, therapy, both, insulin, identify, patient, pain, N0415Z, cannot, cognitive, intervention, syringepen, uses, correct, code, issues, pillbox, independent, medications, syringes, filled, manipulate, assessment, N0415A
group_guidance:
SECTION N - MEDICATIONS CODING GUIDANCE (N0415 ONLY)

---
CORE PRINCIPLES

1. HIGH-RISK DRUGS = By pharmacological class, not indication
2. Code by two-column structure: Column 1 (Is Taking) and Column 2 (Indication Noted)
3. Indication required for all high-risk drugs taken (for Column 2 = 1)

NOTE: M-items (M2001, M2003, M2010, M2020, M2030) are coded separately in group_018

---
N0415 - HIGH-RISK DRUG CLASSES: USE AND INDICATION (TWO-COLUMN STRUCTURE)

CRITICAL: N0415 uses TWO separate columns that must be coded independently.

COLUMN 1: Is Taking (Item codes ending in '1': N0415A1, N0415E1, etc.)
- Code 1 if patient currently taking ANY medication in that class
- Code 0 if not taking any medications in that class

COLUMN 2: Indication Noted (Item codes ending in '2': N0415A2, N0415E2, etc.)
- Code 1 ONLY if Column 1 = 1 AND indication documented for ALL medications in that class
- Code 0 if Column 1 = 0 OR indication not documented for all medications
- MUST check Column 1 before coding Column 2

DRUG CLASSES (6 classes × 2 columns = 12 items):
A = Antipsychotic (N0415A1, N0415A2)
E = Anticoagulant (N0415E1, N0415E2)
F = Antibiotic (N0415F1, N0415F2)
H = Opioid (N0415H1, N0415H2)
I = Antiplatelet (N0415I1, N0415I2)
J = Hypoglycemic including insulin (N0415J1, N0415J2)
Z = None of the above (N0415Z - single item)

CODING RULES:

DEPENDENCY RULE:
- N0415x2 can ONLY = 1 if N0415x1 = 1
- If N0415x1 = 0, then N0415x2 MUST = 0

"ALL MEDICATIONS" RULE:
- N0415x2 = 1 ONLY if indication documented for EVERY medication in that class
- If patient on multiple meds in same class, ALL must have documented indications

MUTUAL EXCLUSIVITY:
- N0415Z = 1 ONLY if patient taking NO medications at all
- If N0415Z = 1, ALL N0415x1 items must = 0
- If ANY N0415x1 = 1, then N0415Z must = 0

PHARMACOLOGICAL CLASSIFICATION (not how medication is used):
- Aspirin = Antiplatelet (I), even if used for pain
- Quetiapine = Antipsychotic (A), even if used for sleep  
- Gabapentin = NOT opioid (not H), even if used for pain

CODING EXAMPLES:

Example 1: Patient on warfarin with indication
Clinical: "Warfarin 5mg daily for atrial fibrillation"
→ N0415E1 = 1 (taking anticoagulant)
→ N0415E2 = 1 (indication documented)

Example 2: Patient on warfarin without indication
Clinical: "Warfarin 5mg daily"
→ N0415E1 = 1 (taking anticoagulant)  
→ N0415E2 = 0 (indication not documented)

Example 3: Multiple antipsychotics, partial indication
Clinical: "Quetiapine 100mg HS, Risperidone 1mg for agitation"
→ N0415A1 = 1 (taking antipsychotics)
→ N0415A2 = 0 (indication for Risperidone but NOT Quetiapine - need ALL)

Example 4: No medications
Clinical: "Patient not on any medications"
→ All N0415x1 = 0, All N0415x2 = 0, N0415Z = 1

---
DRUG CLASS DEFINITIONS

A. ANTIPSYCHOTIC:
First-generation (typical): Haloperidol, chlorpromazine, fluphenazine
Second-generation (atypical): Quetiapine, risperidone, olanzapine, aripiprazole, ziprasidone
Used for: Schizophrenia, bipolar, psychosis, agitation, off-label sleep/anxiety

E. ANTICOAGULANT:
Warfarin (Coumadin)
Direct oral anticoagulants (DOACs): Apixaban, rivaroxaban, dabigatran, edoxaban
Heparins: Enoxaparin, heparin
Used for: DVT/PE, atrial fibrillation, mechanical heart valves, stroke prevention

F. ANTIBIOTIC:
Any antimicrobial agent for bacterial infections
Includes: Penicillins, cephalosporins, fluoroquinolones, macrolides, sulfonamides, etc.
Does NOT include: Antivirals, antifungals (unless systemic for infection)

H. OPIOID:
Natural: Morphine, codeine
Semi-synthetic: Hydrocodone, oxycodone, hydromorphone, oxymorphone
Synthetic: Fentanyl, tramadol, methadone
Combination products: Percocet, Vicodin, Tylenol #3
Does NOT include: Gabapentin, pregabalin (not opioids)

I. ANTIPLATELET:
Aspirin (any dose used for cardiac/stroke prevention)
P2Y12 inhibitors: Clopidogrel, ticagrelor, prasugrel
Dipyridamole
Used for: CAD, stroke prevention, post-stent, peripheral arterial disease

J. HYPOGLYCEMIC (including insulin):
Insulin: All types (rapid, short, intermediate, long-acting)
Oral agents: Metformin, sulfonylureas, DPP-4 inhibitors, SGLT2 inhibitors, GLP-1 agonists
Used for: Diabetes management

---
COMMON CODING ERRORS

N0415:
❌ Coding N0415x2 = 1 when N0415x1 = 0 → Violates dependency rule
❌ Coding N0415x2 = 1 when indication documented for SOME (not ALL) → Need ALL medications
❌ Coding aspirin as opioid → Aspirin is antiplatelet
❌ Missing antibiotic patient is currently taking → Review all medications
❌ Coding N0415Z = 1 with other N0415x1 = 1 → Z is mutually exclusive

---
QUICK REFERENCE

N0415 DRUG CLASSES (TWO-COLUMN STRUCTURE):
A1 = Antipsychotic - Is Taking | A2 = Indication Noted
E1 = Anticoagulant - Is Taking | E2 = Indication Noted
F1 = Antibiotic - Is Taking | F2 = Indication Noted
H1 = Opioid - Is Taking | H2 = Indication Noted
I1 = Antiplatelet - Is Taking | I2 = Indication Noted
J1 = Hypoglycemic/insulin - Is Taking | J2 = Indication Noted
Z = None (mutually exclusive with all x1 items)

---
END GUIDANCE
llm_prompt_template:
You are an Expert OASIS-E1 Clinician specializing in high-risk medication classification. Code these items: {field_codes}
Return JSON array only.

---
CLINICAL SUMMARY: {clinician_summary}

PATIENT CONTEXT:
{patient_data}

MEDICATION LIST:
{medication}

---
INFERENCE RULES

PERMITTED (medium/low confidence):
- Drug classes from medication names (warfarin → anticoagulant, morphine → opioid)
- Indication from "Warfarin for AFib", "Insulin for diabetes"

PROHIBITED:
- Assuming indication without documentation
- Guessing medication class without knowing drug
- Coding by how medication is used (code by pharmacological class)

DRUG CLASS INFERENCE:
Warfarin, apixaban, rivaroxaban → Anticoagulant (E)
Cipro, Bactrim, amoxicillin → Antibiotic (F)
Morphine, oxycodone, hydrocodone → Opioid (H)
Aspirin, clopidogrel → Antiplatelet (I)
Insulin, metformin, glipizide → Hypoglycemic (J)
Quetiapine, risperidone, haloperidol → Antipsychotic (A)

---
GUIDANCE: {oasis_guidance}

---
CODING PROTOCOL

N0415 HIGH-RISK DRUGS (TWO-COLUMN STRUCTURE - 13 ITEMS TOTAL):

CRITICAL: Each drug class has TWO separate item codes that must be coded independently.

STRUCTURE:
- Column 1 (Is Taking): Item codes ending in '1' (N0415A1, N0415E1, N0415F1, N0415H1, N0415I1, N0415J1)
- Column 2 (Indication Noted): Item codes ending in '2' (N0415A2, N0415E2, N0415F2, N0415H2, N0415I2, N0415J2)
- None of above: N0415Z (single item)

DRUG CLASSES:

A = Antipsychotic
  N0415A1: Code 1 if taking ANY antipsychotic, 0 if not
  N0415A2: Code 1 ONLY if N0415A1=1 AND indication documented for ALL antipsychotics, otherwise 0

E = Anticoagulant
  N0415E1: Code 1 if taking ANY anticoagulant, 0 if not
  N0415E2: Code 1 ONLY if N0415E1=1 AND indication documented for ALL anticoagulants, otherwise 0

F = Antibiotic
  N0415F1: Code 1 if taking ANY antibiotic, 0 if not
  N0415F2: Code 1 ONLY if N0415F1=1 AND indication documented for ALL antibiotics, otherwise 0

H = Opioid
  N0415H1: Code 1 if taking ANY opioid, 0 if not
  N0415H2: Code 1 ONLY if N0415H1=1 AND indication documented for ALL opioids, otherwise 0

I = Antiplatelet
  N0415I1: Code 1 if taking ANY antiplatelet, 0 if not
  N0415I2: Code 1 ONLY if N0415I1=1 AND indication documented for ALL antiplatelets, otherwise 0

J = Hypoglycemic (including insulin)
  N0415J1: Code 1 if taking ANY hypoglycemic or insulin, 0 if not
  N0415J2: Code 1 ONLY if N0415J1=1 AND indication documented for ALL hypoglycemics, otherwise 0

Z = None of the above
  N0415Z: Code 1 ONLY if patient taking NO medications at all (mutually exclusive with all N0415x1)

CRITICAL CODING RULES:

1. DEPENDENCY RULE:
   - N0415x2 can ONLY = 1 if N0415x1 = 1
   - If N0415x1 = 0, then N0415x2 MUST = 0
   - Example: N0415A1=0 → N0415A2 MUST = 0

2. "ALL MEDICATIONS" RULE:
   - N0415x2 = 1 ONLY if indication documented for EVERY medication in that class
   - If patient on multiple meds in same class, ALL must have documented indications
   - Example: Patient on Warfarin (indication: AFib) + Aspirin (no indication)
     → N0415E1=1, N0415E2=1 (Warfarin has indication)
     → N0415I1=1, N0415I2=0 (Aspirin no indication)

3. MUTUAL EXCLUSIVITY:
   - If N0415Z = 1, ALL N0415x1 items must = 0
   - If ANY N0415x1 = 1, then N0415Z must = 0

4. CODE VALUES:
   - Use "1" for checked/yes (string)
   - Use "0" for not checked/no (string)

5. PHARMACOLOGICAL CLASSIFICATION:
   - Code by drug class, NOT by how medication is used
   - Quetiapine = Antipsychotic even if used for sleep
   - Aspirin = Antiplatelet even if used for pain

CODING EXAMPLES:

Example 1: Warfarin with indication
Clinical: "Warfarin 5mg daily for atrial fibrillation"
→ N0415E1 = "1", N0415E2 = "1"

Example 2: Warfarin without indication
Clinical: "Warfarin 5mg daily"
→ N0415E1 = "1", N0415E2 = "0"

Example 3: Multiple antipsychotics, partial indication
Clinical: "Quetiapine 100mg HS, Risperidone 1mg for agitation"
→ N0415A1 = "1", N0415A2 = "0" (Quetiapine lacks indication - need ALL)

Example 4: No medications
Clinical: "Patient not on any medications"
→ All N0415x1 = "0", All N0415x2 = "0", N0415Z = "1"

---
OUTPUT FORMAT

[
  {
    "item_code": "N0415A1",
    "value": "0",
    "rationale": "Quote: '...' [Patient not taking antipsychotic medications OR list medication names if taking]",
    "confidence": "high|medium|low"
  },
  {
    "item_code": "N0415A2",
    "value": "0",
    "rationale": "Quote: '...' [If N0415A1=0: 'N0415A1=0, therefore N0415A2 must be 0 per dependency rule.' If N0415A1=1: State whether indications documented for ALL antipsychotics]",
    "confidence": "high|medium|low"
  },
  
  [Continue similar structure for E1, E2, F1, F2, H1, H2, I1, I2, J1, J2]
  
  {
    "item_code": "N0415Z",
    "value": "0|1",
    "rationale": "Quote: '...' [Patient is/is not on any medications. If any N0415x1=1, Z must=0 per mutual exclusivity]",
    "confidence": "high"
  }
]

SPECIAL NOTES:
- N0415: Each class has TWO separate item codes (x1 for Is Taking, x2 for Indication Noted)
- N0415x2 can ONLY = 1 if N0415x1 = 1 (dependency rule)
- N0415x2 requires indication documented for ALL drugs in that class
- N0415Z mutually exclusive with all N0415x1 (only if no meds)
- Code by pharmacological class, not indication (quetiapine = antipsychotic even if for sleep)

---

rag_query: medication, caregiver, anxiety, swallow, surgical, prefilled, therapy, both, M2003, insulin, identify, patient, pain, cannot, cognitive, intervention, syringepen, uses, correct, code, M2001, issues, pillbox, independent, M2030, medications, syringes, filled, manipulate, assessment
group_guidance:
SECTION N - MEDICATIONS CODING GUIDANCE

---
CORE PRINCIPLES

1. HIGH-RISK DRUGS = By pharmacological class, not indication
2. DRUG REGIMEN REVIEW = Complete medication assessment for issues
3. MEDICATION MANAGEMENT = Current ability to self-administer
4. CODE ACTUAL ABILITY, not willingness/compliance
5. Indication required for all high-risk drugs taken

---
N0415 - HIGH-RISK DRUG CLASSES: USE AND INDICATION

Two-column format:
Column 1: Is patient taking medication in this class? (check if yes)
Column 2: Is indication noted for ALL medications in this class? (check if yes in column 1)

DRUG CLASSES:
A = Antipsychotic
E = Anticoagulant
F = Antibiotic
H = Opioid
I = Antiplatelet
J = Hypoglycemic (including insulin)
Z = None of the above

CODING RULES:
- Column 1: Check if patient currently taking ANY medication in that class
- Column 2: Check ONLY if indication documented for ALL meds in that class
- Z: Check ONLY if patient taking NO medications at all (mutually exclusive)
- Multiple classes can be checked (except Z)

PHARMACOLOGICAL CLASSIFICATION:
Code by drug class, NOT how it's used:
- Aspirin = Antiplatelet (I), even if used for pain
- Quetiapine = Antipsychotic (A), even if used for sleep
- Gabapentin = NOT opioid (not H), even if used for pain

COLUMN 1 PATTERNS:
"Takes Seroquel" → Check A (antipsychotic)
"On warfarin" → Check E (anticoagulant)
"Completing antibiotic course" → Check F
"Morphine for pain" → Check H (opioid)
"Daily aspirin" → Check I (antiplatelet)
"Insulin and metformin" → Check J (hypoglycemic)
"No medications" → Check Z only

COLUMN 2 - INDICATION NOTED:
Check ONLY if indication documented for ALL drugs in that class
"Warfarin for atrial fibrillation" → Indication noted, check column 2
"Takes Seroquel" without indication → Do NOT check column 2
Multiple drugs in class: ALL must have indications to check column 2

---
DRUG CLASS DEFINITIONS

A. ANTIPSYCHOTIC:
First-generation (typical): Haloperidol, chlorpromazine, fluphenazine
Second-generation (atypical): Quetiapine, risperidone, olanzapine, aripiprazole, ziprasidone
Used for: Schizophrenia, bipolar, psychosis, agitation, off-label sleep/anxiety

E. ANTICOAGULANT:
Warfarin (Coumadin)
Direct oral anticoagulants (DOACs): Apixaban, rivaroxaban, dabigatran, edoxaban
Heparins: Enoxaparin, heparin
Used for: DVT/PE, atrial fibrillation, mechanical heart valves, stroke prevention

F. ANTIBIOTIC:
Any antimicrobial agent for bacterial infections
Includes: Penicillins, cephalosporins, fluoroquinolones, macrolides, sulfonamides, etc.
Does NOT include: Antivirals, antifungals (unless systemic for infection)

H. OPIOID:
Natural: Morphine, codeine
Semi-synthetic: Hydrocodone, oxycodone, hydromorphone, oxymorphone
Synthetic: Fentanyl, tramadol, methadone
Combination products: Percocet, Vicodin, Tylenol #3
Does NOT include: Gabapentin, pregabalin (not opioids)

I. ANTIPLATELET:
Aspirin (any dose used for cardiac/stroke prevention)
P2Y12 inhibitors: Clopidogrel, ticagrelor, prasugrel
Dipyridamole
Used for: CAD, stroke prevention, post-stent, peripheral arterial disease

J. HYPOGLYCEMIC (including insulin):
Insulin: All types (rapid, short, intermediate, long-acting)
Oral agents: Metformin, sulfonylureas, DPP-4 inhibitors, SGLT2 inhibitors, GLP-1 agonists
Used for: Diabetes management

---
M2001 - DRUG REGIMEN REVIEW

"Did a complete drug regimen review identify potential clinically significant medication issues?"

CODES:
0 = No - No issues found → Skip to M2010
1 = Yes - Issues found → Continue to M2003
9 = NA - Patient not taking any medications → Skip to O0110 (next section)

DEFINITION:
Complete drug regimen review = Systematic assessment of ALL medications for:
- Drug interactions
- Duplications
- Allergies/contraindications
- Inappropriate dosing
- Adverse effects
- Non-adherence issues
- Omissions (missing needed medications)

CLINICALLY SIGNIFICANT = Issues requiring physician notification/intervention

DECISION:
Patient takes medications?
- NO → Code 9, skip to next section
- YES → Review completed, issues found?
  - NO → Code 0, skip to M2010
  - YES → Code 1, continue to M2003

ISSUES INCLUDE:
- Drug-drug interactions (warfarin + aspirin without indication)
- Therapeutic duplications (two NSAIDs, two anticoagulants)
- Medication without indication
- Contraindicated medications
- Dosing errors (dose too high/low)
- Missing necessary medications (no PPI with dual antiplatelet therapy)
- Adverse drug reactions

PATTERNS:
Code 0: "Medication review completed, no issues identified"
Code 1: "Review identified warfarin-aspirin interaction, MD notified"
Code 9: "Patient not on any medications"

---
M2003 - MEDICATION FOLLOW-UP

Only complete if M2001 = 1 (issues found)

"Did the agency contact physician by midnight of next calendar day and complete prescribed/recommended actions?"

CODES:
0 = No
1 = Yes

REQUIREMENTS:
- Contact physician (or designee) by midnight of NEXT calendar day
- Complete prescribed/recommended actions in response to issues

TIMELINE:
Issues identified on Monday → Must contact MD by Tuesday midnight
Issues identified on Friday → Must contact MD by Saturday midnight

ACTIONS INCLUDE:
- Medication discontinued
- Dose adjusted
- New medication ordered
- Laboratory tests ordered
- Monitoring plan established

PATTERNS:
Code 0: "MD not contacted yet" or "Contacted but actions not completed"
Code 1: "MD contacted same day, warfarin held per MD order"

---
M2010 - PATIENT/CAREGIVER HIGH-RISK DRUG EDUCATION

"Has patient/caregiver received instruction on special precautions for all high-risk medications and how/when to report problems?"

CODES:
0 = No
1 = Yes
NA = Patient not taking any high-risk drugs OR patient/caregiver fully knowledgeable

HIGH-RISK DRUGS = Those checked in N0415 (columns A, E, F, H, I, J)

EDUCATION TOPICS:
- Special precautions for each drug
- Side effects to watch for
- When to contact provider
- Storage requirements
- Administration techniques
- Drug interactions to avoid
- Monitoring requirements (labs, vital signs)

DECISION:
Patient on high-risk drugs (any checked in N0415 A, E, F, H, I, J)?
- NO → Code NA
- YES → Patient/caregiver received full education?
  - Already fully knowledgeable → Code NA
  - Yes, education provided → Code 1
  - No, education not yet provided → Code 0

PATTERNS:
Code 0: "Education planned for next visit"
Code 1: "Warfarin education completed, patient verbalizes understanding"
Code NA: "Not on high-risk drugs" OR "Patient already knowledgeable about insulin management"

---
M2020 - MANAGEMENT OF ORAL MEDICATIONS

"Patient's current ability to prepare and take all oral medications reliably and safely"

NOTE: This is ABILITY, not compliance/willingness. Excludes injectable and IV medications.

CODES:
0 = Able to independently take correct medication(s) and proper dosage(s) at correct times
1 = Able to take medication(s) at correct times if:
    a. individual dosages prepared in advance by another person; OR
    b. another person develops drug diary or chart
2 = Able to take medication(s) at correct times if given reminders by another person at appropriate times
3 = Unable to take medication unless administered by another person
NA = No oral medications prescribed

DECISION TREE:

Step 1: Takes oral medications?
NO → Code NA

Step 2: Can take independently (gets pills, takes correct ones at correct times)?
YES → Code 0

Step 3: Needs help organizing but takes independently once set up?
YES → Code 1 (pillbox filled by helper, or med chart provided)

Step 4: Needs reminders when to take meds but can self-administer?
YES → Code 2 (helper reminds, patient takes)

Step 5: Helper must administer medications?
YES → Code 3 (cannot self-administer)

CODE DEFINITIONS:

CODE 0 - INDEPENDENT:
- Gets pills from bottles
- Identifies correct medications
- Takes correct doses
- Takes at correct times
- No assistance needed

CODE 1 - SETUP ASSISTANCE:
- Helper prepares pillbox/med organizer
OR
- Helper creates medication schedule/chart
- Patient then takes meds independently from prepared system
- No reminders needed for timing

CODE 2 - REMINDER ASSISTANCE:
- Patient can self-administer
- Needs reminders WHEN to take
- Helper cues: "Time for your pills"
- Patient physically takes own medications

CODE 3 - DEPENDENT:
- Helper administers all medications
- Patient cannot/does not self-administer
- Helper gives pills to patient, may supervise swallowing

PATTERNS:
Code 0: "Manages all oral medications independently"
Code 1: "Daughter fills weekly pillbox, patient takes from organizer"
Code 2: "Needs reminders to take meds, then takes independently"
Code 3: "Nurse administers all oral medications"
Code NA: "No oral medications"

COGNITIVE IMPAIRMENT:
- Forgets to take meds without reminders → Code 2
- Cannot identify correct pills even with setup → Code 3
- Uses pillbox filled by family → Code 1

PHYSICAL LIMITATIONS:
- Cannot open bottles due to arthritis, uses prefilled organizer → Code 1
- Cannot swallow pills, needs crushing/assistance → Code 3

---
M2030 - MANAGEMENT OF INJECTABLE MEDICATIONS

"Patient's current ability to prepare and take all prescribed injectable medications reliably and safely"

NOTE: Excludes IV medications. Includes subcutaneous, intramuscular injections.

CODES:
0 = Able to independently take correct medication(s) and proper dosage(s) at correct times
1 = Able to take injectable medication(s) at correct times if:
    a. individual syringes prepared in advance by another person; OR
    b. another person develops drug diary or chart
2 = Able to take medication(s) at correct times if given reminders by another person based on frequency
3 = Unable to take injectable medication unless administered by another person
NA = No injectable medications prescribed

DECISION TREE:

Step 1: Takes injectable medications?
NO → Code NA

Step 2: Can prepare and administer independently?
YES → Code 0

Step 3: Needs prefilled syringes but injects independently?
YES → Code 1 (helper prefills, patient injects)

Step 4: Needs reminders when to inject but can self-administer?
YES → Code 2 (helper reminds, patient injects)

Step 5: Helper must administer injections?
YES → Code 3

CODE DEFINITIONS:

CODE 0 - INDEPENDENT:
- Draws up medication (or uses prefilled pen)
- Prepares injection site
- Administers injection
- Disposes of sharps appropriately
- Performs at correct times

CODE 1 - PREFILLED ASSISTANCE:
- Helper prefills syringes in advance
OR
- Helper creates injection schedule
- Patient administers from prefilled syringes
- Patient knows when to inject

CODE 2 - REMINDER ASSISTANCE:
- Patient can prepare and administer
- Needs reminders about timing/frequency
- Helper cues when injection due
- Patient performs injection

CODE 3 - DEPENDENT:
- Helper administers all injections
- Patient cannot/does not self-inject
- Includes: IM injections by nurse, subcutaneous by caregiver

COMMON INJECTABLES:
- Insulin (subcutaneous)
- Enoxaparin/heparin (subcutaneous anticoagulants)
- Biologics (subcutaneous immunosuppressants)
- B12 injections (intramuscular)
- Hormone injections

PATTERNS:
Code 0: "Self-administers insulin injections independently"
Code 1: "Prefilled insulin pens, patient injects"
Code 2: "Needs reminders for Lovenox timing, then self-injects"
Code 3: "Nurse administers insulin injections"
Code NA: "No injectable medications"

INSULIN MANAGEMENT:
- Draws up and injects correctly → Code 0
- Uses insulin pen, dials and injects → Code 0
- Prefilled syringes by family, patient injects → Code 1
- Sliding scale, needs help determining dose → Code 3
- Cannot manipulate syringe/pen → Code 3

---
MEDICATION MANAGEMENT INTEGRATION

ORAL vs INJECTABLE:
- Code separately
- Can be different levels (independent with oral, dependent on injectable)
- Both NA if patient on IV medications only

CONSISTENCY RULES:
- If M2020 = NA (no oral meds) but patient on medications → Must have injectable or IV
- If M2030 = NA (no injectable) but patient on meds → Must have oral or IV
- If both NA → Patient likely on IV only or no medications

ABILITY vs COMPLIANCE:
- Code ABILITY (CAN they do it?)
- NOT willingness/compliance (DO they do it?)
- If patient can but refuses → Code ability level, document refusal separately

COGNITIVE + PHYSICAL:
Consider both:
- Cognitively impaired, needs setup and reminders → Code lower level
- Physically unable to manipulate → Code 3
- Both issues → Code lowest applicable

---
COMMON SCENARIOS

SCENARIO 1: Complex medication regimen
- Multiple oral meds, daughter fills weekly pillbox
- Patient takes from organizer independently
- No injectable medications
Coding: M2020=1, M2030=NA

SCENARIO 2: Insulin-dependent diabetic
- Oral meds: Independent
- Insulin: Self-injects with prefilled pens
Coding: M2020=0, M2030=0

SCENARIO 3: Dementia patient
- Oral meds: Needs reminders, takes independently when prompted
- No injectables
Coding: M2020=2, M2030=NA

SCENARIO 4: Post-surgical with Lovenox
- Oral pain meds: Takes independently
- Lovenox: Nurse administers subcutaneous injections
Coding: M2020=0, M2030=3

SCENARIO 5: Total medication dependence
- Oral meds: Staff administers all
- Insulin: Nurse administers all injections
Coding: M2020=3, M2030=3

---
COMMON CODING ERRORS

N0415:
❌ Checking column 2 without indication documented → Need documented indication
❌ Coding aspirin as opioid → Aspirin is antiplatelet
❌ Missing antibiotic that patient is currently taking → Review all medications
❌ Checking Z with other classes → Z is mutually exclusive

M2001/M2003:
❌ Coding "0" when issues exist but not acted on → Issues = Code 1
❌ Not coding "9" when patient on no meds → Must use NA code
❌ Coding M2003 without M2001=1 → Only complete if issues found

M2010:
❌ Coding "0" when patient already knowledgeable → Should be NA
❌ Not checking if on high-risk drugs → Cross-reference N0415

M2020/M2030:
❌ Coding compliance instead of ability → Code what patient CAN do
❌ Coding "1" for reminders → Code 1 is setup, Code 2 is reminders
❌ Not coding separately for oral vs injectable → Independent codes
❌ Coding both NA when patient on medications → At least one should be coded

---
QUICK REFERENCE

N0415 DRUG CLASSES:
A = Antipsychotic
E = Anticoagulant
F = Antibiotic
H = Opioid
I = Antiplatelet
J = Hypoglycemic/insulin
Z = None (mutually exclusive)

M2001 REVIEW:
0 = No issues → Skip to M2010
1 = Issues found → Continue to M2003
9 = No meds → Skip to next section

M2003 FOLLOW-UP (if M2001=1):
0 = No contact/action
1 = Contacted MD + actions completed

M2010 EDUCATION:
0 = No
1 = Yes
NA = No high-risk drugs OR already knowledgeable

MEDICATION MANAGEMENT:
0 = Independent
1 = Setup/prefilled assistance
2 = Reminder assistance
3 = Dependent (administered by helper)
NA = No medications in that category

---
END GUIDANCE
llm_prompt_template:
You are an Expert OASIS-E1 Clinician specializing in medication management assessment. Code these items: {field_codes}
Return JSON array only.

---
CLINICAL SUMMARY: {clinician_summary}

PATIENT CONTEXT:
{patient_data}

MEDICATION LIST:
{medication}

---
INFERENCE RULES

PERMITTED (medium/low confidence):
- Drug classes from medication names (warfarin → anticoagulant, morphine → opioid)
- Management ability from "Takes own medications", "Family fills pillbox", "Nurse gives all meds"
- Education from "Teaching completed", "Patient verbalizes understanding"
- Drug issues from "Interaction noted", "Duplication identified"
- Indication from "Warfarin for AFib", "Insulin for diabetes"

PROHIBITED:
- Assuming indication without documentation
- Guessing medication class without knowing drug
- Coding compliance as ability
- Assuming independence from absence of mention
- Coding education complete without documentation

DRUG CLASS INFERENCE:
Warfarin, apixaban, rivaroxaban → Anticoagulant (E)
Cipro, Bactrim, amoxicillin → Antibiotic (F)
Morphine, oxycodone, hydrocodone → Opioid (H)
Aspirin, clopidogrel → Antiplatelet (I)
Insulin, metformin, glipizide → Hypoglycemic (J)
Quetiapine, risperidone, haloperidol → Antipsychotic (A)

ABILITY INFERENCE:
"Manages medications independently" → M2020/M2030 = 0
"Weekly pillbox filled by daughter, patient takes daily" → M2020 = 1
"Needs reminders to take meds" → M2020 = 2
"Nurse administers all medications" → M2020/M2030 = 3

---
GUIDANCE: {oasis_guidance}

---
CODING PROTOCOL

N0415 HIGH-RISK DRUGS (two columns per class):
Review medication list for each class:
A-Antipsychotic: Check column 1 if taking. Check column 2 if indication noted for ALL.
E-Anticoagulant: Check column 1 if taking. Check column 2 if indication noted for ALL.
F-Antibiotic: Check column 1 if taking. Check column 2 if indication noted for ALL.
H-Opioid: Check column 1 if taking. Check column 2 if indication noted for ALL.
I-Antiplatelet: Check column 1 if taking. Check column 2 if indication noted for ALL.
J-Hypoglycemic: Check column 1 if taking. Check column 2 if indication noted for ALL.
Z-None: Check ONLY if no medications at all (mutually exclusive with A-J).

Return format: {"A": {"taking": true/false, "indication_noted": true/false}, ...}

M2001 DRUG REGIMEN REVIEW:
Patient on medications?
- NO → Code 9, skip to next section
- YES → Complete review done, issues found?
  - NO → Code 0, skip to M2010
  - YES → Code 1, continue to M2003

Issues = interactions, duplications, contraindications, dosing errors, omissions

M2003 MEDICATION FOLLOW-UP (only if M2001=1):
Physician contacted by midnight next day AND actions completed?
- NO → Code 0
- YES → Code 1

M2010 HIGH-RISK DRUG EDUCATION:
Patient on high-risk drugs (any N0415 A, E, F, H, I, J checked)?
- NO → Code NA
- YES → Patient/caregiver received education OR already fully knowledgeable?
  - Already knowledgeable → Code NA
  - Education completed → Code 1
  - Not yet educated → Code 0

M2020 ORAL MEDICATION MANAGEMENT (ability, not compliance):
No oral meds → Code NA
Can take independently (correct meds, doses, times) → Code 0
Needs pillbox filled or chart made, then independent → Code 1
Needs reminders when to take, then takes independently → Code 2
Helper administers all → Code 3

M2030 INJECTABLE MEDICATION MANAGEMENT (ability, not compliance):
No injectable meds → Code NA
Can prepare and inject independently → Code 0
Needs syringes prefilled or chart, then injects independently → Code 1
Needs reminders when to inject, then injects independently → Code 2
Helper administers all injections → Code 3

VALIDATION:
- N0415: Column 2 checked only if column 1 checked AND indication documented
- N0415: Z not checked with A-J
- M2001=9 if no meds → Skip M2003, M2010, may affect M2020/M2030
- M2003 only if M2001=1
- M2010 NA if no high-risk drugs
- M2020/M2030: Code ability (CAN do), not compliance (DOES do)

---
OUTPUT FORMAT

[
  {
    "item_code": "N0415A|N0415E|N0415F|N0415H|N0415I|N0415J|N0415Z|M2001|M2003|M2010|M2020|M2030",
    "value": "N0415: {taking: true/false, indication_noted: true/false} | M2001: 0/1/9 | M2003: 0/1 | M2010: 0/1/NA | M2020/M2030: 0/1/2/3/NA",
    "rationale": "Quote: '...' [For N0415: medication names and indications if documented. For M2001: issues identified or none. For M2020/M2030: description of ability. State if inferred.]",
    "confidence": "high|medium|low"
  }
]

SPECIAL NOTES:
- N0415: Code by pharmacological class, not indication (quetiapine = antipsychotic even if for sleep)
- N0415: Column 2 requires indication for ALL drugs in that class
- N0415: Z mutually exclusive with A-J (only if no meds)
- M2001=9 → Patient on no medications, skip to next section
- M2001=0 → Skip M2003, go to M2010
- M2003: Only code if M2001=1
- M2010: NA if no high-risk drugs OR if already fully knowledgeable
- M2020/M2030: Code 1 = setup/prefilled, Code 2 = reminders (not same)
- M2020/M2030: Code ABILITY (can they?), not compliance (do they?)
- If both M2020 and M2030 are NA, patient likely on IV meds only or no meds

---
VALIDATION CHECKLIST:
✓ Valid JSON, no markdown
✓ {field_count} objects
✓ N0415: Each class has taking and indication_noted fields
✓ N0415: Column 2 (indication_noted) = true only if column 1 (taking) = true
✓ N0415: Z not true if any A-J true
✓ N0415: Drug classes correctly identified from medication names
✓ M2001: Code 9 if no meds, skip M2003/M2010/possibly M2020/M2030
✓ M2001: Code 0 → M2003 not completed
✓ M2003: Only completed if M2001=1
✓ M2010: NA if no high-risk drugs
✓ M2020: Coded for oral med ability (0/1/2/3/NA)
✓ M2030: Coded for injectable med ability (0/1/2/3/NA)
✓ M2020/M2030: Distinguish Code 1 (setup) from Code 2 (reminders)
✓ Evidence supports all medication identifications and ability codes

Return JSON only.

group_guidance:
SECTION O: SPECIAL TREATMENT, PROCEDURES, AND PROGRAMS GUIDANCE

O0110. SPECIAL TREATMENTS, PROCEDURES, AND PROGRAMS

Check all that apply ON ADMISSION

CANCER TREATMENTS:
A1. Chemotherapy (parent category)
  A2. IV chemotherapy
  A3. Oral chemotherapy
  A10. Other chemotherapy
B1. Radiation

RESPIRATORY THERAPIES:
C1. Oxygen Therapy (parent category)
  C2. Continuous oxygen
  C3. Intermittent oxygen
  C4. High-concentration oxygen
D1. Suctioning (parent category)
  D2. Scheduled suctioning
  D3. As needed suctioning
E1. Tracheostomy care
F1. Invasive Mechanical Ventilator
G1. Non-invasive Mechanical Ventilator (parent category)
  G2. BiPAP
  G3. CPAP

OTHER TREATMENTS:
H1. IV Medications (parent category)
  H2. Vasoactive medications
  H3. Antibiotics (IV)
  H4. Anticoagulation (IV)
  H10. Other IV medications
I1. Transfusions
J1. Dialysis (parent category)
  J2. Hemodialysis
  J3. Peritoneal dialysis
O1. IV Access (parent category)
  O2. Peripheral IV
  O3. Mid-line IV
  O4. Central line (PICC, tunneled, port)

NONE OF THE ABOVE:
Z1. None of the Above

CODING RULES:
- Check parent category AND specific subcategory
- Check all that apply
- Z1 mutually exclusive with all others
- Code what is present on day of admission/SOC

llm_prompt_template:
You are an Expert OASIS-E1 Clinician. Code these items: {field_codes}
Return JSON array only.

---
CLINICAL SUMMARY: {clinician_summary}

PATIENT CONTEXT:
{patient_data}

---
INFERENCE RULES

PERMITTED (medium/low confidence):
- Treatments from "Receiving IV antibiotics", "On oxygen 2L continuous", "Hemodialysis 3x/week"
- Routes from medication administration descriptions
- Device presence from "PICC line in place", "Peripheral IV", "Tracheostomy"
- Frequency from "PRN", "scheduled q4h", "continuous"
- Types from medication/device names

PROHIBITED:
- Assuming treatments without documentation
- Coding completed treatments as current
- Coding planned treatments not yet started
- Guessing routes without clear documentation
- Coding historical devices no longer present

TREATMENT INFERENCE:
"IV Vancomycin" → H1=true, H3=true
"Oxygen 2L nasal cannula continuous" → C1=true, C2=true
"PICC line" → O1=true, O4=true
"Hemodialysis" → J1=true, J2=true
"Receiving chemotherapy" → A1=true (determine route)

---
GUIDANCE: {oasis_guidance}

---
CODING PROTOCOL

O0110 SPECIAL TREATMENTS ON ADMISSION (check all that apply):

rag_query: vital signs blood pressure temperature pulse respiratory rate oxygen saturation SpO2 pain height weight BMI orthostatic lying sitting standing apical radial rhythm
llm_prompt_template:
You are an Expert Home Health Assessment Clinician extracting Non-OASIS supplemental data fields.

TASK: Extract data for the following Non-OASIS fields: {field_codes}
Return a JSON array with one object per field.

CLINICAL SUMMARY:
{clinical_summary}

FIELD DEFINITIONS:

VITAL_SIGNS: Extract ALL vital signs as a SINGLE combined string (item_code MUST be "VITAL_SIGNS").
  Combine all components into one value string, comma-separated. Include:
  BP, HR, RR, Temp, SpO2 with O2 delivery, Pain score with location, Height and Weight if adjacent to VS.
  
  CRITICAL: Return ONE item with item_code="VITAL_SIGNS" containing the full combined string.
  Example: "BP 138/82, HR 88, RR 24, Temp 98.2°F, SpO2 91% on 2L O2 NC"
  DO NOT return separate items for BP, HR, RR, etc. — one item_code="VITAL_SIGNS" only.


CRITICAL EXTRACTION RULES:
1. NEVER return null if ANY relevant clinical data exists — extract what you find, even partial.
2. Include NEGATIVE/NORMAL findings explicitly (e.g., "No visual impairment noted", "No infections identified", "Within normal limits").
3. Include ALL key facts: durations, quantities, dates, severity levels, measurements.
4. For multi-component assessments, address EACH component even if normal/negative.
5. Only return null if the condition is truly NOT mentioned ANYWHERE in the clinical notes.

CRITICAL NULL vs NEGATIVE FINDING RULES:
- NEGATIVE FINDINGS ARE NOT NULL. If the note says "no history of DVT", "no discrepancies", "no abnormalities noted" — these are clinical findings. EXTRACT THEM as values.
- Return null ONLY when the topic is completely UNMENTIONED in the entire clinical note — no reference at all.
- "No X noted/identified/reported" = extract that finding. "Topic not mentioned anywhere" = null.

EXTRACTION PROTOCOL:
1. Search clinical summary for ALL relevant data for each field
2. Extract exact values, measurements, and descriptions
3. If data is partially available, extract what exists and note gaps
4. If no data found for a field, set value to null

NOW: Extract data for {field_codes}. Return JSON array only.

CRITICAL: Do NOT add severity qualifiers (severe, moderate, mild) unless they are EXPLICITLY stated in the note. Extract only what is documented.

IMMUNIZATION: List ALL immunizations and vaccinations documented ANYWHERE in the clinical note.
  For EACH vaccine, include: vaccine name + date administered (exact or month/year).

  AGGRESSIVE SEARCH — Check EVERY section of the note:

llm_prompt_template:
You are an Expert Home Health Assessment Clinician extracting Non-OASIS supplemental data fields.

TASK: Extract data for the following Non-OASIS fields: {field_codes}
Return a JSON array with one object per field.

CLINICAL SUMMARY:
{clinical_summary}

FIELD DEFINITIONS:

EYES_VISION: Extract all visual/eye assessment findings EXACTLY as documented. Include:
  - Corrective lenses (glasses for reading/distance, contact lenses — note TYPE)
  - Visual impairments with qualifier (e.g., "No visual impairments affecting safety" — include "affecting safety" if in note)
  - Eye exam status: "Annual eye exam current" or "last eye exam X months ago" — extract this VERBATIM if documented
  - Any eye conditions (glaucoma, cataracts, diabetic retinopathy — extract even if absent: "no diabetic retinopathy")
  
  CRITICAL: 
  - Extract the EXACT wording from the note — "No visual impairments affecting safety" ≠ "No visual impairments documented"
  - If the note says "Annual eye exam current" → extract that EXACTLY
  - Look in HEENT section for eyes assessment
  - DO NOT mix hearing/ear findings with eye findings

FIELD DEFINITIONS:

MUSCULOSKELETAL: Extract musculoskeletal assessment findings. Include:
  - Structural findings (posture, deformities, alignment like "cogwheel rigidity", "forward-flexed posture")
  - Joint status (arthritis, swelling, or explicitly "joints WNL/within normal limits")
  - IMPORTANT: If note states "otherwise joints WNL" or "remaining joints within normal limits" → ALWAYS include this statement
  - Compensatory movements (accessory muscle use for breathing, guarding)
  - Affected vs unaffected sides (e.g., "Left hemiplegia affecting UE and LE. Right side strength 5/5")
  - Active vs passive ROM limitations and their causes
  
  CRITICAL: If only ONE joint/side is affected and the note says the rest are normal/WNL → document BOTH the affected finding AND the "otherwise WNL" statement.
  Example: "Left hip THA, otherwise joints WNL" captures the surgical hip AND normal joints.

MUSCLE_ROM: Extract muscle strength AND range of motion findings. Include:
  - ROM status (full ROM, limited ROM — specify WHICH specific joint is affected, e.g., "right ankle", NOT just "right lower extremity")
  - Strength grades if documented (e.g., "4/5 bilateral lower extremities", "strength 5/5 throughout")
  - Cause of limitations IF stated (e.g., "limited ROM right ankle secondary to infection", "ROM restricted per post-op protocol")
  
  CRITICAL RULES:
  - Do NOT include posture findings (forward-flexed posture, etc.) — those belong in MUSCULOSKELETAL
  - Do NOT include endurance, activity tolerance, or claudication — those belong in CIRCULATORY_HISTORY or MUSCULOSKELETAL
  - Do NOT include gait findings (limp, walker use) — those go in GAIT_EVALUATION
  - Do NOT include TUG test results — those go in TUG_ASSESSMENT
  - Be SPECIFIC about which JOINT (ankle, knee, hip) has limited ROM, not just the extremity
  - Include the REASON for limitation when documented (e.g., "secondary to infection", "per post-op protocol")
  - Include BOTH ROM and strength findings (not endurance)

GAIT_EVALUATION: Describe gait pattern and ambulation ability. Include:
  - Gait pattern description (steady, unsteady, shuffling, forward-flexed)
  - Assistive device used (walker, cane, none)
  - Weight-bearing status (full, partial, non-weight-bearing — specify extremity and orders)
  - Assistance level (independent, minimal/moderate/maximum assist)
  - Compensatory techniques (pursed-lip breathing with activity, energy conservation)
  If patient is non-ambulatory/bedbound, return null — do NOT describe their transfer status.

TUG_ASSESSMENT (complete if fall risk assessment needed): Timed Up and Go test - time in seconds, device used, observations during test, fall risk interpretation (<12s low risk, 12-20s moderate, >20s high), recommendations.

GROOMING_TASKS: Extract the patient's ability to perform GROOMING and personal hygiene tasks specifically. Include:
  - Independence level for grooming/hygiene (independent, minimal assist, moderate assist, dependent)
  - Specific grooming tasks: bathing/showering, dressing (upper vs lower body), oral hygiene, hair care
  - Energy conservation techniques or pacing strategies used FOR GROOMING
  - Adaptive equipment used specifically for grooming/hygiene tasks

  - Return null ONLY when there is truly NO mention of grooming, ADLs, or functional status anywhere in the note

FIELD DEFINITIONS:

HOSPITALIZATION_RISK_ASSESSMENT: Extract the hospitalization risk assessment. Include:
  - Risk level (low, moderate, high)
  - Risk factors contributing to that level (infections, PICC line, wound, falls, etc.)
  - Protective factors if documented
  Format: "Risk level - contributing factors"
  Example: "Moderate risk due to PICC line and active infection. Risk factors: ..."
  CRITICAL: Include BOTH the risk level AND the specific contributing factors.

ACUITY_LEVEL: Assign RED (High) / YELLOW (Moderate) / GREEN (Low) based on: comorbidity count/severity, days since hospitalization, procedure complexity, fall risk, support system, cognitive status, visit frequency needed. Provide rationale for classification.

HOMEBOUND_REASONS: All Medicare homebound criteria met. Document: medical contraindication to leaving, taxing effort required, assistive devices needed, specific conditions (dyspnea, weakness, pain, etc.), mobility limitations with distance in feet, overall characterization (fully homebound/infrequently leaving/leaving with difficulty). Reference objective findings.

PROGNOSIS: Extract the overall prognosis ONLY IF EXPLICITLY DOCUMENTED. Include:
  1. The prognosis LEVEL — use the EXACT word documented (Good/Fair/Guarded/Poor)
  2. The EXPLANATION or contributing factors as documented
  Format: "Level - Explanation" (e.g., "Fair - likely to show improvement with skilled services and medication management").

  CRITICAL RULE 1: ONLY extract if the note explicitly uses a prognosis term like:
  "Prognosis good/fair/guarded/poor" or "Prognosis: fair" or "Prognosis fair with..."
  DO NOT extract "hospitalization risk" language as prognosis — "moderate risk for re-hospitalization" ≠ prognosis.
  DO NOT infer prognosis from clinical conditions — extract ONLY what is explicitly stated.
  
  CRITICAL RULE 2: If NO explicit prognosis statement is found → return null.
  "Guarded" risk ≠ prognosis statement. "High acuity" ≠ prognosis statement.
  Only extract when you find an explicit "Prognosis [word]" statement.
  
  CRITICAL RULE 3: Include ALL details from the explanation — do not shorten it.
  "Fair - likely to show improvement with skilled services and medication management" → extract ALL of it.

ADVANCE_DIRECTIVE: Extract ALL advance directive documentation. Include:
  - Type of directive (living will, healthcare proxy, POA, DNAR/DNI)
  - Designated agent (name, relationship)
  - Document status (on file, copies obtained)
  - Code status (full code, DNAR, DNI)
  - Palliative care involvement if documented
  Search ALL sections including demographics, legal documents, assessment, and plan sections.


CRITICAL EXTRACTION RULES:
1. NEVER return null if ANY relevant clinical data exists — extract what you find, even partial.
2. Include NEGATIVE/NORMAL findings explicitly (e.g., "No visual impairment noted", "No infections identified", "Within normal limits").
3. Include ALL key facts: durations, quantities, dates, severity levels, measurements.
4. For multi-component assessments, address EACH component even if normal/negative.
5. Only return null if the condition is truly NOT mentioned ANYWHERE in the clinical notes.

CRITICAL NULL vs NEGATIVE FINDING RULES:
- NEGATIVE FINDINGS ARE NOT NULL. If the note says "no history of DVT", "no discrepancies", "no abnormalities noted" — these are clinical findings. EXTRACT THEM as values.
- Return null ONLY when the topic is completely UNMENTIONED in the entire clinical note — no reference at all.
- "No X noted/identified/reported" = extract that finding. "Topic not mentioned anywhere" = null.

EXTRACTION PROTOCOL:
1. Search clinical summary for ALL relevant data for each field
2. Extract exact values, measurements, and descriptions
3. If data is partially available, extract what exists and note gaps
4. If no data found for a field, set value to null

NOW: Extract data for {field_codes}. Return JSON array only.

TASK: Extract data for the following Non-OASIS fields: {field_codes}
Return a JSON array with one object per field.

CLINICAL SUMMARY:
{clinical_summary}

FIELD DEFINITIONS:

COMMON_GOALS: Extract the patient's care GOALS — the desired outcomes of care. Include:
  - What is to be achieved (wound healing, improved mobility, pain management, etc.)
  - Measurable targets if stated (SpO2 >88%, maintain blood sugar control)
  - ALL documented goals, including shorter/simpler ones (fall prevention, independence in ADLs, prevent infection)
  
  IMPORTANT: Extract GOALS (what to achieve), NOT interventions/care plan steps (how to achieve it).
  "Wound healing of right heel ulcer" = goal ✓
  "Weekly wound assessment and dressing changes" = intervention ✗
  
  CRITICAL: Extract EVERY goal documented. Common goals often missed:
  - "Prevent falls" or "fall prevention"
  - "Independence in ADLs" or "Return to baseline"
  - "Prevent infection" or "prevent re-hospitalization"
  - "Optimize comfort" (especially palliative patients)
  - "Support ADLs" or "maintain current level of function"
  Look in Goals section AND in Assessment/Plan subsections for goals.

COMMON_INTERVENTIONS: Three categories: Assessment (SOC assessment, VS each visit, fall risk, skin, medication adherence, infection monitoring); Teaching (disease process, medications, fall prevention, infection control, symptom recognition); Care Coordination (physician coordination, DME, labs, interdisciplinary team). Include frequency and expected outcome.
